company.brand_mission: Teaching essential life skills to children, teens, and adults — confidence, resilience, growth mindset, emotional intelligence, and adaptability
company.stage: Scaling ($8M revenue last year, targeting $11M at 15% profitability)
company.market_landscape: Fragmented — lots of small players, no dominant competitor

physical.description: Journals (kids, teens, adults), Conversation Cards, Affirmation Cards, Card Game, Board Game (launching soon)

subscription.description: Membership for parents, educators, therapists, and schools. Includes gated resources, masterclasses, guides, certified parenting coach (DM + live 1:1 calls)
subscription.onboarding: Email sequence over first few weeks

churn_profile.parents: High churn — cite 'price' or 'not for me right now'

subscription.platform: Custom-built (has developer costs that compress subscription margins)

metrics.ltv_cac_gap: 1.56x vs 3.0x benchmark — critically below. Makes profitable paid acquisition scaling impossible.
metrics.seasonality: Q4 holiday gifting is the primary peak

owned_audience.social_strategy: Organic content drives lead magnet downloads to grow email list
owned_audience.note: Massive underutilized asset — only 500-1500 active subscribers despite 600k engaged emails

acquisition.parent_funnel: Meta Ad → physical or digital product purchase → subscription upsell via email (over following weeks)
acquisition.amazon_halo: Amazon performance correlates with Meta performance — they rise and fall together, creating compounding risk

strategic_challenges: LTV:CAC of 1.56x — can't profitably scale paid acquisition as-is
Parent subscription churn is high — members not staying long enough
600k email list with 50% open rate is massively underconverted to subscription
Physical products are mostly one-time buyers — no repeat purchase loop
Revenue is 80% physical (lower margin) — needs to shift toward subscription/digital
Paid social + Amazon performance are correlated — double channel concentration risk
Custom platform developer costs compress subscription margins
No referral program despite a community-oriented product in a word-of-mouth niche
strategic_opportunities: Email list monetization: 600k at 50% open rate = 300k reading every email. Even 1% subscription conversion = 6k new subscribers at zero incremental CAC
Fix parent onboarding: 7-day trial too short; 1:1 coaching access is massively undermarketted and likely underused — people who use the coach will stay
Professional expansion: low churn + organizations have budget + group pricing could be 10x current deal size
Referral program: word-of-mouth is natural in parenting/educator communities; currently untapped
Physical → subscription bridge: Q4 holiday buyers could be converted via post-purchase flows
Raise subscription pricing or restructure tiers: $75/quarter for 1:1 coaching is underpriced
B2B institutional sales: school districts, therapy practices, pediatric offices — much larger deals than individual subscribers
Reduce platform costs: evaluate if custom-built platform vs. Kajabi/Circle/Skool would reduce developer overhead
